meaning: adj.无错误的,无缺点的,完美的,无缺点的,无可指责的
examples: May: and it's been such a faultless performance with the perfect lighting, and music.
Quantum logic may be more suitable to making decisions that work well enough, even if they're not logically faultless.
On the earth, practically it is very difficult to find a piece of literature work which is hundred percent faultless.
Loeb admitted that apart from his mistake earlier in the rally he had enjoyed a faultless run.
The 23-year-old backed up an impressive display against Cardiff with a near faultless performance at Adams Park.
